page: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Additionally, it is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core execution, with stress-ng single-core performance and single-threaded gzip compression both placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth read/write operations fall within the average range. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU testing, ranking in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, LLM inference on small models achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 390.7 tokens per second, though performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% when running large 70B models.

Given its massive memory allocation relative to vCPUs and large local storage capacity, this server offers high resource density for memory-bound applications. The qualitative cost profile reflects a specialized hardware class designed to handle large datasets without the overhead of excess compute cores. It is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, caching layers, and data-intensive applications requiring substantial local storage and fast single-threaded processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz, and a massive 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing 32.0 GB of memory per core. The server is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression, alongside strong PassMark CPU and memory scores. However, multi-core performance and large-model LLM inference remain average to poor. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and caching systems that require substantial memory capacity and local storage without requiring a high density of compute cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-heavy workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring massive RAM capacity and extensive local storage for demanding database and data-intensive workloads.
